title: coryphopteris indochinensis
intro: Coryphopteris indochinensis, also known as the Indochina leather fern, is a species of fern native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Coryphopteris indochinensis include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant is typically prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
